findings.title: Skill does not specify a license
Over-broad description and keyword baiting
findings.description: The skill description contains an extensive list of trigger phrases ('automation recommendations', 'optimize their Claude Code setup', 'improving Claude Code workflows', 'first set up Claude Code', 'what Claude Code features they should use') designed to maximize activation across a wide range of user queries. This is keyword baiting to increase the skill's invocation rate.
findings.evidence: description: Analyze a codebase and recommend Claude Code automations (hooks, subagents, skills, plugins, MCP servers). Use when user asks for automation recommendations, wants to optimize their Claude Code setup, mentions improving Claude Code workflows, asks how to first set up Claude Code for a project, or wants to know what Claude Code features they should use.
findings.remediation: Narrow the description to focus on the core functionality rather than listing every possible trigger phrase.

findings.title: Indirect prompt injection via web search recommendation
findings.description: The skill instructs the agent to 'use web search to find recommendations specific to the codebase's tools, frameworks, and libraries' and similar directives in reference files. This delegates trust to external, uncontrolled web content, which could contain malicious instructions that override the skill's intended behavior.
findings.evidence: Go beyond the reference lists: The reference files contain common patterns, but use web search to find recommendations specific to the codebase's tools, frameworks, and libraries
findings.remediation: Remove instructions to use web search for recommendations, or add explicit warnings about not following instructions from external content.

findings.title: Indirect prompt injection via web search in reference files
findings.description: Multiple reference files (hooks-patterns.md, mcp-servers.md, plugins-reference.md, skills-reference.md, subagent-templates.md) contain instructions to 'use web search to find recommendations specific to the codebase's tools, frameworks, and libraries' or similar. This creates multiple vectors for indirect prompt injection from untrusted web content.
findings.evidence: Note: These are common patterns. Use web search to find hooks for tools/frameworks not listed here to recommend the best hooks for the user.
findings.remediation: Remove web search directives from all reference files. Provide comprehensive static recommendations instead.

findings.title: Tool poisoning via MCP server recommendations
findings.description: The skill recommends installing MCP servers (context7, Playwright, Supabase, GitHub, etc.) that extend the agent's capabilities. While these are legitimate services, the skill's instructions to use web search to find additional MCP servers could lead to recommending malicious or compromised MCP servers from untrusted sources.
findings.evidence: Go beyond the reference lists: The reference files contain common patterns, but use web search to find recommendations specific to the codebase's tools, frameworks, and libraries
findings.remediation: Restrict MCP server recommendations to a curated, verified list. Do not use web search to discover new MCP servers.

findings.title: Tool poisoning via plugin recommendations from web search
findings.description: The plugins-reference.md file instructs to 'use web search to discover additional community plugins.' This could lead to recommending malicious plugins from untrusted sources, potentially introducing backdoors or data exfiltration capabilities.
findings.evidence: Note: These are plugins from the official repository. Use web search to discover additional community plugins.
findings.remediation: Remove web search directive for plugin discovery. Only recommend plugins from the official repository.

findings.title: Tool poisoning via subagent template recommendations from web search
findings.description: The subagent-templates.md file instructs to 'design custom subagents based on the codebase's specific review and analysis needs' and 'use web search to find skill ideas specific to the codebase's tools and frameworks.' This could lead to creating subagents with malicious instructions from web content.
findings.evidence: Note: These are common patterns. Design custom subagents based on the codebase's specific review and analysis needs.
findings.remediation: Remove web search directives. Provide comprehensive static subagent templates instead.

findings.title: Indirect prompt injection via skill recommendations from web search
findings.description: The skills-reference.md file instructs to 'use web search to find skill ideas specific to the codebase's tools and frameworks.' This could lead to creating skills with malicious instructions from untrusted web content.
findings.evidence: Note: These are common patterns. Use web search to find skill ideas specific to the codebase's tools and frameworks.
findings.remediation: Remove web search directive for skill discovery. Provide comprehensive static skill recommendations instead.

findings.title: Over-broad capability claims in reference files
findings.description: The reference files contain extensive lists of recommendations across multiple categories (MCP servers, skills, hooks, subagents, plugins) that could be interpreted as the skill having capabilities beyond its stated purpose of analysis and recommendation.
findings.evidence: Multiple reference files contain detailed instructions for creating skills, hooks, subagents, and plugins, which could be misinterpreted as the skill having implementation capabilities.
findings.remediation: Clarify that the skill only provides recommendations, not implementations.
